core_model.structure_summary: A parametric family of linear programs over couplings with fixed marginals that minimize the integral of a parameter-indexed cost (a concave transform of a pairwise surplus function), where the parameter interpolates between total-surplus maximization and worst-case fairness.

variables.meaning: coupling measure on the product space with prescribed marginals (transport plan)
expected parameter-indexed cost integrated against the coupling
scalar index selecting the concave cost transform (inequality-aversion / aggregation attitude)
two coordinate spaces being matched, with a pairwise surplus/utility kernel between them

notes: The objective is linear in the transport plan (an optimal transport LP), but the cost kernel is a nonlinear concave transform of the surplus and the parametric family is the point of the paper, hence 'mixed' linearity. Object is a constrained optimization over probability couplings with fixed marginals; auxiliary graph/matching probability bounds at the end are not the core structure.
